doc.title: Convert bonds to common stock
doc.event: Holders of Dade Co.'s $500,000 convertible bonds convert them into 40,000 shares of $5-par common. Prepare the conversion entry (book value method).

entries.caption: Convert $500,000 bonds (12,000 unamortized discount) into 40,000 shares of $5 par

lines.label: face off the books
lines.why: The debt leaves at face; the discount line handles the rest of the carrying value.
lines.trap: Bring amortization current to the conversion date FIRST — the 12,000 must be the up-to-date remainder.

lines.label: unamortized remainder
lines.why: The contra account dies with the bond, exactly as in a retirement.
lines.trap: Forgetting this credit overstates the equity recorded by 12,000.

lines.label: 40,000 × $5 par
lines.why: Par discipline survives conversion: shares × par to the stock account.
lines.trap: Market price of the stock on conversion day is IRRELEVANT under the book value method — resist the urge to use it.

lines.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common
lines.label: the plug
lines.why: Carrying value 488,000 − par 200,000 = 288,000. The bonds' book value simply changes clothes into equity.
lines.trap: NO gain or loss, ever, under the book value method — the plug always lands in PIC.

computationPaths.narration: Book value method: equity recorded = the bonds' carrying value. CV = 500,000 − 12,000 = 488,000 → par 200,000 + PIC 288,000.

entries.caption: Convert $500,000 bonds (9,000 unamortized premium) into 40,000 shares of $5 par

lines.label: face
lines.trap: Same ritual as retirement: face, then the premium/discount remainder.

lines.label: unamortized remainder
lines.why: The adjunct account clears on the debit side — the carrying value being converted is 509,000.
lines.trap: Premium DEBITS out; flipping it is the same direction error as in amortization.

lines.label: 40,000 × $5 par
lines.trap: Unchanged by which side the balance sits on.

lines.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common
lines.label: the plug
lines.trap: Bigger carrying value → bigger PIC. The plug tracks CV, not face.

memorize.body: Book value method: equity recorded = bonds' carrying value at conversion (face ± unamortized premium/discount). Common Stock takes par × shares; PIC takes the rest. Gain/loss: never.
Update amortization (and settle accrued interest) to the conversion date BEFORE converting — the carrying value must be current.

questions.prompt: Bonds with face 500,000 and unamortized discount 12,000 convert into 40,000 shares of $5-par common. PIC in Excess of Par is credited for…

distractors.feedback: 300,000 uses FACE (500,000 − 200,000). The book value method transfers the CARRYING value — 488,000 — so PIC gets 488,000 − 200,000 = 288,000.

accountBank.account: Paid-in Capital in Excess of Par — Common

build.scaffold: Retirement mechanics without cash: face off, clear the remaining discount/premium, then the carrying value becomes stock — par first, PIC plug last. If you're reaching for Gain or Cash, stop.
